elements.sent-2411-001: Despite significant advancements in general artificial intelligence, such as GPT-4, their effectiveness in the medical domain (general medical AI, GMAI) remains constrained due to the absence of specialized medical knowledge.
elements.sent-2411-002: To address this challenge, we present GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive multimodal medical dataset created by converting hundreds of specialized medical datasets into meticulously constructed image-text pairs.
elements.sent-2411-003: This dataset features comprehensive task coverage, diverse modalities, and high-quality image-text data.
elements.sent-2411-004: Building upon this multimodal dataset, we propose GMAI-VL, a general medical vision-language model with a progressively three-stage training strategy.
elements.sent-2411-005: This approach significantly enhances the model's ability by integrating visual and textual information, thereby improving its ability to process multimodal data and support accurate diagnosis and clinical decision-making.
elements.sent-2411-006: Experimental evaluations demonstrate that GMAI-VL achieves state-of-the-art results across a wide range of multimodal medical tasks, such as visual question answering and medical image diagnosis.
elements.sent-2411-007: Our contributions include the development of the GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset, the introduction of the GMAI-VL model, and the establishment of new benchmarks in multiple medical domains.
elements.sent-2411-008: Code and dataset will be released at \url{https://github.com/uni-medical/GMAI-VL .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Large-scale Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have rapidly evolved in recent years, effectively integrating visual perception with language understanding by leveraging large-scale multimodal data, which enables them to capture complex visual and textual patterns and drive significant advancements in image recognition, natural language processing, and multimodal tasks.
elements.sent-2411-010: With the advancement of multimodal integration technologies, the demand for high-precision processing of diverse data types in the medical field has become increasingly critical.
elements.sent-2411-011: The ability to effectively integrate and analyze various data modalities, such as medical images, clinical text, and structured clinical records, is pivotal for achieving accurate and comprehensive diagnostic and treatment decisions.
elements.sent-2411-012: However, existing LVLMs, such as GPT-4~\citep{achiam2023gpt , are limited in medical applications due to their lack of domain-specific knowledge, highlighting the need for specialized solutions that effectively integrate medical expertise.}
elements.sent-2411-013: Addressing this challenge requires constructing a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset and developing domain-specific models.
elements.sent-2411-014: For the medical dataset, it should provide high-quality medical knowledge, including the following three aspects: Comprehensive medical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-015: To enhance the model's applicability across various medical scenarios, the dataset should cover a wide range of medical contexts, such as disease types, symptoms, and treatments.
elements.sent-2411-016: Comprehensive task coverage can improve the model's generalization ability and increase its reliability in real-world applications.
elements.sent-2411-017: However, existing models often focus on specific domains~\citep{he2024meddr,li2024integrated,MindChat,Omkar2023XrayGPT,kapadnis2024serpent , limiting their broader applicability.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Expanding the dataset's scope will further enhance the model's utility in clinical practice.. Rich multimodal representation.
elements.sent-2411-019: A well-rounded medical multimodal dataset should encompass various modalities, including different medical imaging types (such as CT, MRI, and X-rays) and diverse forms of textual data (such as medical records and imaging reports).
elements.sent-2411-020: This would allow models to better integrate multi-source information and improve their analytical capabilities.
elements.sent-2411-021: However, existing methods tend to focus on a single type of medical imagery~\citep{johnson2019mimic,wu2023towards,lu2024visual , limiting the model's adaptability to diverse clinical scenarios.}
elements.sent-2411-022: A more diverse multimodal dataset would provide a foundation for developing more comprehensive models, better suited to the complexity of real-world medical environments.

elements.sent-2411-024: High-quality training data is essential for optimizing model performance, especially in medical applications.
elements.sent-2411-025: Ideally, image-text data for medical contexts should consist of a large, well-curated collection of medical images paired with precise textual descriptions.
elements.sent-2411-026: This data would enhance the model's understanding of key medical concepts, such as diagnosis, treatment, and clinical workflows, ultimately contributing to improved clinical outcomes.
elements.sent-2411-027: While progress has been made with datasets sourced from platforms like PubMed~\citep{li2024llava, moor2023med, wu2023towards , these datasets suffer from inconsistent data quality, imprecise alignment between images and text, and a lack of standardization, which limits their effectiveness.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Based on above observations, we propose an annotation-guided data generation methodology for developing a comprehensive multimodal medical datasets.
elements.sent-2411-029: The methodology begins by collecting large-scale, open-source medical imaging datasets and extracting key annotations, such as modality, task type, labels, and bounding boxes.
elements.sent-2411-030: A vision-language model (e.g., GPT-4o) is then used to transform these datasets, covering tasks like lesion detection, segmentation, and disease diagnosis, into high-quality image-text pairs for training LVLMs.
elements.sent-2411-031: To ensure data quality, extracted image information is incorporated into the prompt design, improving model performance across various clinical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-032: This results in a comprehensive multimodal dataset with 5.5M samples, named GMAI-VL-5.5M, which supports the development of general medical LVLMs.
elements.sent-2411-033: Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (a) illustrates the sources, departments, modalities, task types, and instruction formats of the constructed dataset.}
elements.sent-2411-034: With the constructed GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset, we develop a general medical vision-language model, GMAI-VL.
elements.sent-2411-035: To enhance its integration of visual and linguistic features and its instruction-following abilities, a three-stage training strategy is proposed in this paper.
elements.sent-2411-036: Specifically, we sequentially implement shallow and deep alignments in the first two stages, gradually building associations between visual (medical images) and language (medical texts) elements from basic features to high-level semantics.
elements.sent-2411-037: Next, we fine-tune the model with cross-modal instructions, improving its understanding of visual-language interactions and instruction-following in complex tasks.
elements.sent-2411-038: With this strategy, GMAI-VL shows strong performance in medical tasks like visual question answering and medical image diagnosis, providing a solid foundation for advancing multimodal models in the medical field.
elements.sent-2411-039: The contributions of this work are as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-040: We build an annotation-guided data generation methodology to construct GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive vision-language dataset.
elements.sent-2411-041: This dataset offers extensive coverage of medical tasks, diverse multimodal representations, and high-quality image-text pairs, providing a robust foundation for model training.
elements.sent-2411-042: Leveraging GMAI-VL-5.5M, we design GMAI-VL, a versatile medical vision-language model.
elements.sent-2411-043: Our proposed three-stage training strategy enhances its ability to integrate visual and linguistic features, significantly improving its performance in instruction-following and generalization across a wide range of medical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-044: GMAI-VL outperforms existing models in multimodal question-answering tasks, including PMC-VQA and VQA-RAD.
elements.sent-2411-045: It sets new benchmarks on OmniMedVQA, GMAI-MMBench, and Health \& Medicine track of MMMU.
elements.sent-2411-046: Specifically, GMAI-VL achieves an average score of 88.48\% on OmniMedVQA, 62.43\% on the GMAI-MMBench test set, and 51.3\% on the Health \& Medicine track of MMMU.
elements.sent-2411-047: Large-scale medical vision-language datasets of high quality and multiple modalities are the basis of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) in the medical domain.
elements.sent-2411-048: While natural language and vision datasets are easily accessible online, biomedical datasets often focus on text or images only and many of them are limited to specific tasks or modalities, thus with unsatisfactory generalization ability.
elements.sent-2411-049: Notable datasets like MIMIC-CXR \citep{johnson2019mimic and CheXpert \citep{chambon2024chexpert have advanced radiology models but are restricted to single image modality (X-ray), which hinders their use as general-purpose medical LVLMs.}}
elements.sent-2411-050: To address this, researchers have begun scraping public sources like PubMed and textbooks to construct large-scale vision-language datasets.
elements.sent-2411-051: Examples include datasets proposed in LLaVA-Med \citep{li2024llava , Med-Flamingo \citep{moor2023med , and PubMedVision \citep{chen2024huatuogpt , with PubMedVision optimizing LLaVA-Med dataset for higher-quality medical data.}}}
elements.sent-2411-052: In addition to scraping, open-source image datasets with annotations can also be converted into image-text pairs for model training.
elements.sent-2411-053: Specifically, image information like modalities and annotations are input into large language models, e.g., GPT series, to generate text paired with the corresponding image.
elements.sent-2411-054: Some popular examples include the datasets constructed in RadFM \citep{wu2023towards , MedDr \citep{he2024meddr , MedTrinity-25M \citep{xie2024medtrinity , ChiMed-VL \citep{liu2023qilin , BiomedGPT \citep{zhang2024generalist , Med-Gemini \citep{saab2024capabilities , and Med-PaLM \citep{singhal2023large .}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-055: These efforts usually suffer from either limited modalities, data sources, or task coverage.
elements.sent-2411-056: Thus, their dataset quality needs further improvement.
elements.sent-2411-057: To this end, we propose to construct a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset with extensive coverage of medical tasks, diverse multimodal representations, and high-quality image-text pairs, forming a robust foundation for model training.
elements.sent-2411-058: Most of them adapt LVLMs to specific medical applications using specialized medical datasets.
elements.sent-2411-059: For instance, Med-Flamingo~\citep{moor2023med enhances OpenFlamingo-9B using 0.8 million interleaved and 1.6 million paired medical image-text data, highlighting the critical need for multimodal data in medical image analysis and automated report generation tasks.}
elements.sent-2411-060: RadFM~\citep{wu2023towards improves PMC-LLaMA~\citep{wu2023pmc by leveraging 16 million radiology images with text descriptions from diverse sources.}}
elements.sent-2411-061: Similarly, Med-PaLM~\citep{tu2024towards adapts PaLM-E \citep{driess2023palm to the medical domain with approximately one million medical data samples, achieving state-of-the-art performance in diagnostic support and medical knowledge Q\&A.}}
elements.sent-2411-062: LLaVA-Med~\citep{li2024llava utilizes a large-scale biomedical figure-caption dataset extracted from PubMed Central to enhance LLaVA~\citep{touvron2023llama1,touvron2023llama to better understand biomedical images and facilitate open-ended conversational interactions.}}
elements.sent-2411-063: Med-Gemini~\citep{saab2024capabilities leverages long-format question-answering datasets to improve the multimodal and long-contextual capabilities of the baseline Gemini model, enabling superior performance in complex medical Q\&A and multimodal reasoning tasks.}
elements.sent-2411-064: Additionally, HuatuoGPT-Vision~\citep{chen2024huatuogpt and MedDr~\citep{he2024meddr build medical multimodal datasets to adapt general-purpose LVLMs like LLaVA and InternVL to various medical modalities, including radiology, pathology, dermatology, and endoscopy.}}
elements.sent-2411-065: Previous studies usually focus on constructing medical datasets to adapt general-purpose LVLMs but pay less attention to the adaptation strategies.
elements.sent-2411-066: However, naive training/adaptation strategies may not successfully adapt general-purpose LVLMs to the medical data, due to the large gap between the natural image-text pairs and the medical ones.
elements.sent-2411-067: Moreover, these strategies can hardly align the broad imaging modalities and various types of medical text (e.g., prescriptions, radiology reports, and electronic health records) to obtain generalizable features, thus limiting the models' performance.
elements.sent-2411-068: Our work thus proposes a novel three-stage training strategy to better integrate the visual and language features to enhance generalization ability.
elements.sent-2411-069: With rapid advancements in medical vision-language models (VLMs), constructing high-quality datasets is crucial for developing general-purpose models.
elements.sent-2411-070: Unlike previous approaches that primarily rely on published literature, our method utilizes specialized medical datasets to create a more robust, high-quality resource.
elements.sent-2411-071: We build GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset that aggregates data from diverse open-source and proprietary sources.
elements.sent-2411-072: Covering 13 medical imaging modalities and 18 specialties, it addresses a wide range of medical imaging tasks.
elements.sent-2411-073: This dataset enhances the model's ability to process complex medical information, advancing precision medicine and intelligent diagnostics.

elements.sent-2411-074: To construct a comprehensive multimodal medical dataset, we sourced 219 datasets from diverse platforms.
elements.sent-2411-075: Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (a) highlights key data sources, such as Kaggle, Grand Challenge, and Huggingface, which facilitate extensive data collection.}
elements.sent-2411-076: These datasets cover diverse imaging modalities, including fundus, CT, MRI, and ultrasound (US), and span a variety of medical tasks, such as diagnosis, severity assessment, and organ recognition.
elements.sent-2411-077: They also encompass multiple clinical specialties, including pathology, dermatology, ophthalmology, otolaryngology, and oncology, further enhancing their diversity.
elements.sent-2411-078: After data collection, we apply a preprocessing workflow to extract 2D medical images from the videos and 3D medical volumes.
elements.sent-2411-079: For data cleaning, we first extract key annotation information from classification data, including modality, department, and labels for each image, discarding instances with missing or unclear annotations.
elements.sent-2411-080: For segmentation data, we follow the SA-Med2D-20M~\citep{ye2023sa preprocessing method, filtering out low-quality images and labels, and converting them into detection datasets.}
elements.sent-2411-081: The converted segmentation data is then processed together with the original detection dataset, with key annotation information extracted and linked to each image.
elements.sent-2411-082: Finally, the preprocessed data are then standardized and organized into a structured format: <image, modality, label, department, bbox [optional]>.
elements.sent-2411-083: Subsequently, the data are categorized into two primary types: classification datasets and detection/segmentation datasets.
elements.sent-2411-084: Each category is further refined using specific prompts tailored for large model training.
elements.sent-2411-085: For data generation, large vision-language models (GPT-4o) are employed to produce detailed image descriptions and corresponding instruction-following data through annotation-guided data generation methodology.
elements.sent-2411-086: For classification datasets, detailed descriptions of the entire image are generated, while for detection datasets, the focus is on specific regions enclosed by bounding boxes, providing comprehensive functional analyses of these areas.
elements.sent-2411-087: Notably, the segmentation dataset was transformed into a detection dataset using external bounding boxes, and data generation followed detection dataset protocols.
elements.sent-2411-088: Furthermore, to improve the model's multilingual capability, we translated a portion of English image-text data into Chinese.
elements.sent-2411-089: Incorporating multilingual data helps to enhance the generalization capabilities of domain-specific multimodal models.
elements.sent-2411-090: The resulted data is utilized for medical Visual Question Answering (VQA) tasks, forming the comprehensive VQA dataset, named GMAI-VL-5.5M.
elements.sent-2411-091: The detailed pipeline for generating prompt-driven data is illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig:prompt .}

elements.paragraph-2411-006: Accuracy and reliability of generated data.
elements.sent-2411-101: The accuracy and reliability of the generated data are primarily addressed from two key aspects.
elements.sent-2411-102: First, the reliability of the data source is ensured by utilizing high-quality datasets from reputable sources.
elements.sent-2411-103: The data used for generation primarily comes from professional medical challenges (such as Kaggle and GrandChallenge) and publicly available, peer-reviewed datasets.
elements.sent-2411-104: This guarantees the integrity and accuracy of the data at its source.
elements.sent-2411-105: Second, the data generation process is rigorously controlled to ensure accuracy and reliability.
elements.sent-2411-106: Although GPT was used for data generation, prompts were meticulously crafted to include essential annotations, minimizing errors and hallucinations.
elements.sent-2411-107: As illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig:prompt , the proposed annotation-guided approach incorporates specific annotations (e.g., <image, modality, label, department, bbox [optional]>) to produce precise, high-quality descriptions.}
elements.sent-2411-108: Compared to the without-annotation-guided approach, which often results in less detailed outputs, the annotation-guided method delivers more professional, pathology-specific descriptions, significantly enhancing the reliability of the generated data.
elements.paragraph-2411-007: Compared with other medical multimodal datasets.
elements.sent-2411-109: The GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset, as highlighted in Table~\ref{tab:comparison-LVLMs , stands out due to its unmatched scale, encompassing over 5.5 million samples from more than 219 specialized medical imaging datasets.}
elements.sent-2411-110: Unlike other datasets listed, GMAI-VL-5.5M supports a wider variety of modalities and languages, making it a truly global resource that caters to diverse clinical needs.
elements.sent-2411-111: Additionally, GMAI-VL-5.5M emphasizes traceability of its data, ensuring a high standard of clinical relevance and reliability.
elements.sent-2411-112: This comprehensive and diverse dataset is critical for pushing the boundaries of medical multimodal research, enabling more effective training of LVLMs that can generalize across multiple medical tasks and scenarios, thereby driving innovations in precision medicine and intelligent diagnostics.
elements.section-2411-004: GMAI-VL: A General Medical Vision-Language Model
elements.sent-2411-113: The GMAI-VL model is a vision-language model built upon the LLaVA architecture~\citep{liu2023llava,li2024llava , incorporating three key components: a large language model (LLM), a vision encoder, and a projector (MLP), as illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (b).}}
elements.sent-2411-114: These components are designed to work together seamlessly, enabling the model to deliver exceptional performance in medical applications.
elements.sent-2411-115: We adopt InternLM2.5-7B~\citep{team2023internlm as our language processing module, which offers outstanding reasoning capabilities.}
elements.sent-2411-116: With a context window up to one million tokens, it can handle complex medical tasks and generate coherent, accurate responses.
elements.sent-2411-117: Its support for advanced instruction-following makes it particularly effective in addressing intricate medical queries, thereby enhancing the model's ability to understand and respond to a wide range of instructions.
elements.sent-2411-118: For vision processing, GMAI-VL employs a CLIP-based vision encoder~\citep{radford2021learning , which transforms visual inputs into high-dimensional feature representations.}
elements.sent-2411-119: CLIP's strong performance in aligning image and text representations ensures that medical image features are accurately extracted and effectively integrated with linguistic information, significantly enhancing the model's ability to handle multimodal medical data.
elements.sent-2411-120: The MLP, as a projector, serves as a bridge between the vision encoder and the LLM, optimizing high-dimensional outputs and further enhancing feature representation.
elements.sent-2411-121: The seamless integration of these components enables GMAI-VL to excel in processing and understanding complex medical multimodal data.
elements.sent-2411-122: As illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (c), the training process of the GMAI-VL model is divided into three stages: shadow alignment, deep alignment, instruction tuning, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-123: The detailed hyper-parameter settings can be found in Supp.. Mat.
elements.sent-2411-124: To enhance the training of GMAI-VL, we supplement our GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset with additional medical datasets, ultimately forming an 11.7M dataset for training.
elements.sent-2411-125: This supplemented data increases the diversity of training data, exposing the model to a wider range of medical scenarios and visual-language patterns, enhancing its generalization to complex clinical tasks and ensuring robustness in real-world applications.. Fig.~\ref{fig:distribution_of_our_data (in Supp.}
elements.sent-2411-126: Mat.) provides a complete distribution of the utilized datasets during the training stage.
elements.sent-2411-127: The detailed data proportions for each training stage are detailed in Table~\ref{tab:data_ratio (in Supp.. Mat.).}
elements.sent-2411-128: In the shallow alignment phase, we utilize a large-scale medical image-text dataset comprising approximately 11.7 million image-text pairs, sourced from a combination of publicly accessible datasets and proprietary in-house data.
elements.sent-2411-129: To achieve shallow alignment, we freeze both the large language model and the vision encoder, optimizing only the projector.
elements.sent-2411-130: With this optimization stage, the model establishes an initial alignment between medical images and their corresponding textual descriptions.
elements.sent-2411-131: All input images are resized to $336 \times 336$ pixels, and the training objective is to minimize the cross-entropy loss of the text tokens.
elements.sent-2411-132: Most vision encoders in multimodal models are pre-trained on natural images, so it is essential to address the domain gap between medical and natural images during the deep alignment stage.
elements.sent-2411-133: To achieve this, we fine-tune both the vision-language projector and the vision encoder, enhancing the alignment between the visual features of medical images and the language model's feature space.
elements.sent-2411-134: At this stage, we fine-tune our GMAI-VL model (including the vision encoder, the language model, and the projector components) by instruction tuning to enhance its instruction-following and dialogue capabilities.
elements.sent-2411-135: The multimodal instruction tuning data is primarily derived from the training data in previous stages, with a filtering process to select high-quality and more suitable data for fine-tuning.
elements.sent-2411-136: Specifically, we manually reviewed a subset of each dataset and labeled it as either “high quality” or “low quality.” High-quality datasets were used for tuning, while most low-quality ones were excluded, except for a small portion retained for diversity.
elements.sent-2411-137: This approach allowed efficient filtering without reviewing every sample.
elements.sent-2411-138: Additionally, we incorporate medical text dialogue data to ensure the model's versatility in handling various dialogue scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-139: Thus, our instruction tuning data comprises approximately ten million samples.
elements.sent-2411-140: To evaluate our model, we employed several established multimodal medical benchmarks, each targeting specific aspects of medical image understanding and question answering.
elements.sent-2411-141: Below is a brief overview of the benchmarks used in our experiments:.
elements.sent-2411-142: Traditional medical VQA benchmarks: Traditional multimodal medical question-answering benchmarks, such as VQA-RAD~\citep{lau2018dataset , SLAKE~\citep{liu2021slake , and PMCVQA~\citep{zhang2023pmc , span various imaging modalities tasks, primarily assessing the model's ability to extract information from medical images and answer clinical questions.}}}
elements.sent-2411-143: They evaluate the model's performance in understanding medical imaging and integrating multimodal information.
elements.sent-2411-144: OmniMedVQA: OmniMedVQA~\citep{hu2024omnimedvqa provides a rich dataset of paired medical images and text, designed to evaluate the model's ability to recognize and understand fundamental medical imaging concepts, focusing on cross-modal reasoning and information integration.}
elements.sent-2411-145: GMAI-MMBench: GMAI-MMBench~\citep{chen2024gmai focuses on assessing the model's ability to identify fine-grained objects in complex clinical scenarios, challenging its capacity to handle long-context tasks and accurately recognize and reason over detailed medical features.}
elements.sent-2411-146: MMMU Health \& Medicine track: The Health \& Medicine track of the MMMU~\citep{yue2024mmmu benchmark spans a wide range of medical fields, derived from university exams, quizzes, and textbooks.}
elements.sent-2411-147: It evaluates the model's reasoning ability in complex medical scenarios and the specialized knowledge in health and medicine.

elements.sent-2411-153: Table~\ref{tab:omni_result summarizes the performance of various large vision-language models (LVLMs), including our proposed GMAI-VL, across five question types: Modality Recognition, Anatomy Identification, Disease Diagnosis, Lesion Grading, and Other Biological Attributes.}
elements.sent-2411-154: GMAI-VL demonstrates outstanding accuracy across multiple tasks, achieving 98.64\% in Modality Recognition, 92.95\% in Anatomy Identification, and 88.71\% in Disease Diagnosis.
elements.sent-2411-155: It outperforms both open-source LVLMs and medical-specific models, underscoring its capability to accurately identify anatomical structures and diagnose diseases from visual data.
elements.sent-2411-156: In Lesion Grading, GMAI-VL attained the highest score of 87.21\%, and it also delivers a strong performance of 82.95\% in Other Biological Attributes, showcasing its versatility across diverse biological contexts.
elements.sent-2411-157: With an average accuracy of 88.48\%, the highest among all evaluated models, GMAI-VL excels not only in general medical question-answer tasks but also in complex reasoning requiring domain-specific knowledge, surpassing models like HuatuoGPT-Vision-34B and InternVL2-40B.
elements.sent-2411-158: These results verify our GMAI-VL is a leading model in multimodal medical image understanding, setting a new benchmark for medical VQA tasks.
elements.sent-2411-159: Its consistent top performance across question types highlights its potential for broader applications in medical question answering.
elements.sent-2411-160: The GMAI-MMBench benchmark is a comprehensive medical multimodal benchmark designed to evaluate models on a range of clinical visual question-answering (VQA) tasks.
elements.sent-2411-161: Table~\ref{clincial_VQA_results presents the results of various LVLMs, including open-source LVLMs and commercial models, evaluated on the val and test sets across multiple clinical tasks.}
elements.sent-2411-162: Notably, GMAI-VL excels in specific tasks such as abnormality recognition (73.78\%), biological variation recognition (63.06\%), and clinical disease diagnosis (66.67\%).
elements.sent-2411-163: These results demonstrates the model's strong ability in understanding and interpreting complex clinical images.
elements.sent-2411-164: In comparison to other models, GMAI-VL consistently achieves either the best or second-best performance across most tasks.
elements.sent-2411-165: For instance, it ranks first in 16 out of 20 categories, including key tasks such as AR (Attribute Recognition) and DD (Disease Diagnosis), where it achieved scores of 75.26\% and 67.14\%, respectively, suggesting GMAI-VL's strength in understanding medical scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-166: Overall, GMAI-VL establishes a new benchmark in various clinical VQA tasks, demonstrating its potential as a reliable and versatile tool in medical multimodal applications.
elements.sent-2411-167: The MMMU benchmark, a widely recognized standard for evaluating multimodal models, was used to assess the performance of our proposed GMAI-VL model on the Health \& Medicine track.
elements.sent-2411-168: The experimental results in Table~\ref{tab:mmmu_result show the model's performance across five key categories: Basic Medical Science (BMS), Clinical Medicine (CM), Diagnostics and Laboratory Medicine (DLM), Pharmacy (P), and Public Health (PH).}

elements.sent-2411-170: These results highlight the model's proficiency in handling complex tasks requiring diagnostic reasoning, pharmaceutical knowledge, and public health expertise.
elements.sent-2411-171: In BMS, GMAI-VL scores 50.0\%, achieve the best performance, demonstrating the model's the capacity of understanding medical knowledge.
elements.sent-2411-172: In CM, the model achieves 60.0\%, remaining competitive with other leading models.
elements.sent-2411-173: These results underscore the model's ability in processing both clinical and foundational medical information effectively.
elements.sent-2411-174: Overall, GMAI-VL achieves an average score of 51.3\% across the Health \& Medicine track, which is a top performance among other models, verifying its versatility in specialized medical domains.

elements.sent-2411-177: In the VQA-RAD, SLAKE, and PMC-VQA benchmarks, removing GMAI-VL resulted in an average performance improvement of 8.6\%.
elements.sent-2411-178: For the OmniMedVQA, GMAI-MMBench, and MMMU Health \& Medicine track, the improvements were 8.52\%, 6.75\%, and 5.3\%, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-179: These results demonstrate that the GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset provides highly accurate and reliable medical knowledge, particularly in recognizing and understanding multimodal medical data, which significantly boosts model performance.
elements.sent-2411-180: This underscores the dataset's diversity, comprehensiveness, and its ability to effectively complement other datasets in complex medical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-181: In this study, we develop GMAI-VL, a large vision-language model, along with GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive multimodal medical dataset aimed at advancing general medical AI (GMAI).
elements.sent-2411-182: GMAI-VL-5.5M, which converts hundreds of medical image analysis datasets into high-quality image-text pairs through annotation-guided data generation, enables GMAI-VL to effectively address a wide range of clinical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-183: Experimental results show that GMAI-VL-5.5M significantly enhances GMAI-VL's performance on diverse clinical tasks, achieving state-of-the-art results across several key benchmarks.

elements.sent-2411-200: During this stage, both the large language model (LLM) and the vision encoder are frozen, while the MLP remains trainable.
elements.sent-2411-201: The learning rate is set to \(1e^{-3}\) with a cosine decay schedule, using AdamW as the optimizer.
elements.sent-2411-202: Input size is \(336 \times 336\), and the total batch size is \(32 \times 8 \times 2\).
elements.sent-2411-203: Here, 32 refers to the number of GPUs used, 8 represents the micro-batch size per GPU, and 2 is the number of gradient accumulation steps.
elements.sent-2411-204: Notably, we use a soft packing technique, which allows each sample to actually contain multiple sequences—estimated at over 2 per sample.
elements.sent-2411-206: The LLM remains frozen, while the vision encoder and MLP are unfrozen and trainable, allowing for deeper feature alignment.
elements.sent-2411-207: The learning rate is lowered to \(1e^{-4}\), the total batch size is \(32 \times 4 \times 4\), and other settings remain consistent with Stage I.
elements.sent-2411-209: In this stage, all components of the model are trainable, allowing for comprehensive fine-tuning.
elements.sent-2411-210: The learning rate is further reduced to \(1e^{-5}\), the total batch size is \(32 \times 4 \times 4\), while other parameters, including optimizer, remain unchanged across stages.
elements.sent-2411-211: Each stage utilizes DeepSpeed for mixed-precision training (bf16) on 32 A100 (80GB) GPUs.
elements.sent-2411-212: To accelerate the training rate of the model, we utilize the soft packing method in xtuner\footnote{https://github.com/InternLM/xtuner , which packs multiple sequences into each sample to optimize memory usage and efficiency..}

elements.sent-2411-252: Despite significant advancements in general AI, its effectiveness in the medical domain is limited by the lack of specialized medical knowledge.
elements.sent-2411-253: To address this, we formulate GMAI-VL-5.5M, a multimodal medical dataset created by converting hundreds of specialized medical datasets with various annotations into high-quality image-text pairs.
elements.sent-2411-254: This dataset offers comprehensive task coverage, diverse modalities, and rich image-text data.
elements.sent-2411-255: Building upon this dataset, we develop GMAI-VL, a general medical vision-language model, with a three-stage training strategy that enhances the integration of visual and textual information.
elements.sent-2411-256: This approach significantly improves the model's ability to process multimodal data, supporting accurate diagnoses and clinical decision-making.
elements.sent-2411-257: Experiments show that GMAI-VL achieves state-of-the-art performance across various multimodal medical tasks, including visual question answering and medical image diagnosis.
elements.sent-2411-258: Recent advancements in Large-scale Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have driven progress in image recognition, natural language processing, and multimodal tasks, leveraging the power of multimodal datasets.
elements.sent-2411-259: In the medical field (general medical AI, GMAI), as these technologies mature, the need for accurate processing of diverse data—such as medical images, clinical text, and structured records—has become critical for reliable diagnostic and treatment decisions.
elements.sent-2411-260: However, existing LVLMs, like GPT-4o~\citep{achiam2023gpt , face limitations in medical applications due to their lack of domain-specific knowledge.}
elements.sent-2411-261: This highlights the need for specialized solutions that incorporate medical expertise.
elements.sent-2411-262: To address this, we develop a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset and corresponding domain-specific models.
elements.sent-2411-263: As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (a), Our dataset provides high-quality medical knowledge across three aspects: (i) Comprehensive medical tasks: To improve the model's applicability in various medical scenarios, our dataset covers a wide range of disease types, symptoms, treatments, and medical workflows.}
elements.sent-2411-264: We use the well-curated collection of medical images paired with precise textual descriptions to build our dataset.

elements.sent-2411-265: To achieve this, we develop an annotation-guided data generation process to create this large-scale multimodal medical dataset.
elements.sent-2411-266: First, we collect multiple open-source medical imaging datasets and extract their key annotations (e.g., modality, task type, labels, bounding boxes).
elements.sent-2411-267: Next, we convert these well-labeled annotations into text descriptions suitable for training vision-language models.
elements.sent-2411-268: This ensures that the medical image-text pairs are accurately constructed, with annotations verified by medical experts in the collected medical imaging datasets.
elements.sent-2411-269: The process results in the GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset, consisting of 5.5 million samples, which supports the development of general medical LVLMs.
elements.sent-2411-270: Building upon this dataset, we develop a general medical vision-language model, GMAI-VL.
elements.sent-2411-271: To optimize its ability to integrate visual and linguistic features and follow complex instructions, we present a three-stage training strategy.
elements.sent-2411-272: The first two stages involve shallow and deep alignments, gradually establishing connections between medical images and texts, from basic features to high-level semantics.
elements.sent-2411-273: In the final stage, the model is fine-tuned with cross-modal instructions, improving its understanding of visual-language interactions and its ability to follow intricate tasks, as shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:pipeline (b)\&(c).}

elements.sent-2411-276: We develop an annotation-guided data generation methodology to create GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset with diverse medical tasks, rich multimodal representations, and high-quality image-text pairs.
elements.sent-2411-277: Leveraging this dataset, we design GMAI-VL, a general medical vision-language model, and propose a three-stage training strategy to enhance its integration of visual and linguistic features, improving its performance in various medical tasks.
elements.sent-2411-278: We demonstrate that GMAI-VL outperforms existing models in multimodal question-answering tasks, setting new benchmarks with high performance on OmniMedVQA, GMAI-MMBench, and the Health \& Medicine track of MMMU.

elements.figure-2411-019: \caption{The examples of annotation-guided prompts for paired data format generation.} \label{tab:prompts} \begin{tabular}{p{\textwidth}} \multicolumn{1}{c}{Instruction-following data prompt} \begin{minipage}{1.0\textwidth} \begin{minipage}{0.2\textwidth} \includegraphics[width=0.95\linewidth]{figs/retinal.png} \end{minipage} \begin{minipage}{0.8\textwidth} As a medical expert, you will receive a \textless fundus\textgreater image belonging to \textless Ophthalmology\textgreater, which is labeled as \textless moderate nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy\textgreater. Please generate question-answer pairs based on the following requirements: 1. The question-answer pairs must be strictly based on the content of the image and directly related to the label information, should not state known medical facts or general definitions directly. 2. Focus on reasoning around the label and guide observation of the visible features of the disease, such as affected areas, symptoms, and changes in the retina. 3. Each question and answer must be precise, clear, and derived from observable characteristics in the image, with no vague or uncertain information. 4. The output format must follow the standard JSON format with the structure as follows: \{ 'question': \textless question content \textgreater , 'answer': \textless answer content \textgreater \} \end{minipage} \end{minipage} Question Can we see any hard exudates or fluid leakage in the image? Answer The image does show some bright, yellowish deposits, which are consistent with hard exudates, often seen in moderate nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy due to fluid leakage. Question What is the appearance of the optic disc in this retinal image? Answer The optic disc appears normal without any visible signs of swelling or abnormal changes, suggesting that the optic nerve is not yet affected by the disease. \multicolumn{1}{c}{Caption data prompt} \begin{minipage}{1.0\textwidth} \begin{minipage}{0.2\textwidth} \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figs/det_sample.png} \end{minipage} \begin{minipage}{0.8\textwidth} As a medical expert, you are given a \textless X-ray\textgreater\ image belonging to \textless Pulmonary Medicine\textgreater, where the \textless bbox [179,164,500,434]\textgreater\ is labeled as \textless Pneumothorax\textgreater. Please generate a very detailed description related to bbox in the given image. When generating the description, you must strictly adhere to the following guidelines: 1. The generated description should be inferable from the image; 2. The generated description should include image features related to the given label; 3. The generated description should start with 'This region'; 4. The description may imply known information but should not directly state known information; 5. The generated description should include major entities within and around the region, background elements, texture features, and any other notable features. \end{minipage} \end{minipage} Output: This region shows ... the right hemithorax on a frontal chest X-ray, ... an area suggestive of abnormal air accumulation. The bounded area appears less dense compared to surrounding lung tissue, ... The edges of the lung are visible, separated from the chest wall by a faint radiolucent (darker) space, suggestive of pneumothorax. The lung markings are notably absent within this area, ... The texture within the defined area has a homogeneous, darkened appearance consistent with air rather than tissue density, contrasting with the granular pattern in the aerated lung parenchyma outside this region. \end{tabular}
elements.section-2411-013: GMAI-VL-5.5M: A Comprehensive Multimodal Medical Dataset
elements.sent-2411-300: With rapid advancements in medical vision-language models, constructing high-quality datasets is essential for developing general-purpose models.
elements.sent-2411-301: Unlike previous approaches that mainly rely on published literature, our method leverages specialized medical datasets with various annotations to create a more robust, high-quality resource.
elements.sent-2411-302: We present GMAI-VL-5.5M, a comprehensive medical vision-language dataset that aggregates data from diverse open-source and proprietary sources.
elements.sent-2411-303: Covering 13 medical imaging modalities and 18 specialties, it supports a wide range of medical imaging tasks.
elements.sent-2411-304: After data collection, we follow a workflow to pair medical data (including both 2D and 3D data) with corresponding text annotations.
elements.sent-2411-305: To ensure high-quality annotations, we first extract key information from the annotations provided by medical experts.
elements.sent-2411-306: For classification data, we extract the modality, department, and labels for each image, discarding instances with missing or unclear annotations.
elements.sent-2411-307: For segmentation data, we follow the SA-Med2D-20M~\citep{ye2023sa approach, filtering out low-quality images and labels, and converting them into detection annotation format.}
elements.sent-2411-308: Finally, the preprocessed data is standardized and organized into a structured format: <image, modality, label, department, bbox [optional]>, where ``bbox'' refers to the bounding box locations for detection annotations.
elements.sent-2411-309: To generate paired visual medical data with text descriptions, we use large vision-language models (i.e., GPT-4o) to produce detailed descriptions with instructions via an annotation-guided methodology.
elements.sent-2411-310: In details, for classification datasets, comprehensive descriptions of the entire image are created, while for detection datasets, the focus is on specific regions enclosed by bounding boxes, with detailed functional analyses of these areas.
elements.sent-2411-311: Furthermore, based on the given information, instruction-following question-answer pairs related to the medical images are generated.
elements.sent-2411-312: These pairs involve specific instructions tailored to the medical context, such as identifying critical anatomical or pathological features within the medical images (e.g., tumors, lesions, or organs) or providing in-depth interpretations of regions of interest.
elements.sent-2411-313: These instructions guide the model to produce relevant, precise responses, thereby enhancing its applicability to specialized medical imaging tasks.
elements.sent-2411-314: As mentioned in the previous subsection, the segmentation dataset is transformed into a detection format using external bounding boxes, and data generation follows the detection dataset protocols.
elements.sent-2411-315: The detailed example prompts are shown in Table~\ref{tab:prompts .}
elements.sent-2411-316: The generated data is then used for medical Visual Question Answering (VQA) tasks, forming the comprehensive VQA dataset, GMAI-VL-5.5M.
elements.sent-2411-317: To enhance the model's multilingual capability, we translate approximately 30\% of the English image-text data into Chinese.
elements.sent-2411-318: This multilingual data helps further improve the generalization ability of domain-specific multimodal models.. \if 0.
elements.sent-2411-319: Our dataset encompasses a broad spectrum of medical imaging tasks and modalities, forming a robust foundation for the development and evaluation of medical LVLMs.
elements.sent-2411-320: Table~\ref{tab:distribution summarizes the distribution of key modalities, tasks, clinical departments, and clinical tasks within GMAI-VL-5.5M, illustrating its diversity and extensive coverage.}
elements.sent-2411-321: For a more detailed analysis, additional visual insights into the dataset composition can be found in the supplementary material.
elements.sent-2411-322: The quality of the generated paired data and annotations is ensured through two main approaches.
elements.sent-2411-323: First, we use high-quality datasets from trusted sources, including professional challenges like Kaggle and GrandChallenge, as well as peer-reviewed datasets, ensuring data accuracy and reliability.
elements.sent-2411-324: Second, we carefully control the data generation process.
elements.sent-2411-325: While GPT is used for generation, the prompts are designed with essential annotations (e.g., <image, modality, label, department, bbox [optional]>) to minimize errors.
elements.sent-2411-326: This annotation-guided approach produces more detailed and professional descriptions.
elements.sent-2411-327: The data quality is detailed in the supplementary material.

elements.sent-2411-328: The GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset, as shown in Table~\ref{tab:comparison-LVLMs , distinguishes itself with its unmatched scale, featuring over 5.5 million samples from more than 219 specialized medical imaging datasets.}
elements.sent-2411-329: Unlike other datasets, GMAI-VL-5.5M supports a broader range of modalities and languages, making it a truly global resource that addresses diverse clinical needs.
elements.sent-2411-330: Furthermore, GMAI-VL-5.5M ensures data traceability, maintaining high clinical relevance and reliability.
elements.sent-2411-331: This extensive and varied dataset is crucial for advancing medical multimodal research, enabling more effective training of LVLMs that can generalize across numerous medical tasks and scenarios, ultimately driving innovations in precision medicine and intelligent diagnostics.
elements.sent-2411-332: We use InternLM2.5-7B~\citep{team2023internlm as our language processing module, which provides exceptional reasoning capabilities.}
elements.sent-2411-333: With a context window of up to one million tokens, it can manage complex medical tasks and generate coherent, accurate responses.
elements.sent-2411-334: For vision processing, we adopt a CLIP-based vision encoder~\citep{radford2021learning , which converts visual inputs into high-dimensional feature representations.}
elements.sent-2411-335: The MLP projector bridges the vision encoder and language processing module, optimizing high-dimensional outputs and improving feature representation.
elements.sent-2411-336: This framework enables GMAI-VL to effectively process multimodal medical data.
elements.sent-2411-337: As shown in Fig.\ref{fig:pipeline (c), the training process of the GMAI-VL model is divided into three stages: shallow alignment, deep alignment, and instruction tuning.}
elements.sent-2411-338: To enhance the training of GMAI-VL, we supplement the GMAI-VL-5.5M dataset with additional medical datasets, resulting in a total training dataset of 11.7M samples.
elements.sent-2411-339: In this phase, we use a large-scale medical image-text dataset of approximately 11.7 million image-text pairs.
elements.sent-2411-340: During this stage, we freeze both the large language model and the vision encoder, optimizing only the projector.
elements.sent-2411-341: This optimization establishes an initial alignment between medical images and their corresponding textual descriptions.
elements.sent-2411-342: During training, (i) the objective is to minimize the cross-entropy loss of the text tokens; (ii) all images are resized to $336 \times 336$ pixels; (iii) the learning rate is set to \(1e^{-3}\) with a cosine decay schedule, and AdamW is used as the optimizer; (iv) the total batch size is \(32 \times 8 \times 2\), where 32 refers to the number of GPUs used, eight represents the micro-batch size per GPU, and two is the number of gradient accumulation steps; and (v) a soft packing technique is used to allow each sample to contain multiple sequences, averaging over two sequences per sample.
elements.sent-2411-343: Most vision encoders in multimodal models are pre-trained on natural images, so addressing the domain gap between medical and natural images is critical during the deep alignment stage.
elements.sent-2411-344: To bridge this gap, we fine-tune both the vision-language projector and the vision encoder, enhancing the alignment between the visual features of medical images and the language model's feature space.
elements.sent-2411-345: The learning rate is set to \(1e^{-4}\), the total batch size is \(32 \times 4 \times 4\), and other settings remain consistent with Stage I.
elements.sent-2411-346: In this stage, we fine-tune the entire GMAI-VL model—vision encoder, language model, and projector—through instruction tuning to enhance its instruction-following and dialogue capabilities.
elements.sent-2411-347: The multimodal instruction data is primarily derived from the previous stages.
elements.sent-2411-348: Note that this stage discards the low-quality samples that with too short descriptions or extremely lower confidence in the previous stages.
elements.sent-2411-349: Additionally, medical text dialogue data is incorporated to improve the model's handling of various dialogue scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-350: This results in ten million samples for instruction tuning.
elements.sent-2411-351: During taining, the learning rate is set to \(1e^{-5}\), the total batch size is \(32 \times 4 \times 4\), while other parameters, including optimizer, remain unchanged across stages.

elements.sent-2411-354: Detailed information about these benchmarks can be found in the supplementary material.

elements.figure-2411-032: \caption{Prompt for Evaluating the Quality of Medical Image-Text Dialogues} \label{tab:quality} \begin{tabular}{|p{\textwidth}|} You are a medical imaging expert tasked with scoring each medical text-image dialogue according to the following criteria. The score range is from 1 to 5, where a lower score indicates higher dialogue quality. Each dialogue should be evaluated based on the following criteria, considering language clarity, completeness, medical accuracy, and relevance to medical imaging. Below is an example of the dialogue content and related information: - Modality: \textless image modality\textgreater (e.g., CT, MRI, X-ray, etc.) - Department: \textless department\textgreater (e.g., Ophthalmology, Pulmonary etc.) - Label: \textless label\textgreater (e.g., The pre-defined label belong to image) - Dialogue Content: \quad - Question: \textless question\textgreater Question content\textless/question\textgreater \quad - Answer: \textless answer\textgreater Answer content\textless /answer\textgreater Please score each dialogue based on the following dimensions: 1. Medical Information Accuracy: \quad - Ensure that medical terminology, diagnostic processes, and treatment plans align with current medical standards and practices. \quad - Assess whether there are any medical errors or misleading information that could impact diagnostic or treatment decisions. \quad - Example: If an imaging description or medical terminology is incorrect, how does this affect the overall evaluation? 2. Language Clarity and Fluency: \quad - Evaluate whether the dialogue is concise, clear, and easy to understand. \quad - Assess whether the language is fluent and natural, and if there are any overly complex sentence structures that might hinder information transmission. \quad - Example: Are there any unnecessary words or overly complex structures that slow down the understanding of the content? 3. Dialogue Completeness: \quad - Evaluate whether the dialogue includes complete medical information, covering the patient's history, imaging results, diagnostic information, and any necessary details. \quad - Assess whether any essential information is missing, omitted, or incomplete. \quad - Example: If imaging results are not mentioned or the information is incomplete, does this affect the overall evaluation? 4. Medical Imaging Relevance: \quad - Evaluate whether the analysis of medical imaging information is thorough and accurate. \quad - Ensure that the imaging data is appropriately interpreted, the descriptions are clear and accurate, and they align with the medical diagnosis. \quad - Assess whether the imaging data effectively supports or supplements other medical information. \quad - Example: If the description of the imaging data is inaccurate or incomplete, does it compromise the reliability of the diagnostic outcome? Scoring Criteria: - 1: The dialogue is concise, accurate, and clear, fully conforming to medical standards. The imaging interpretation is appropriate, the information is complete and accurate, and it fully supports the medical diagnosis. - 2: The dialogue is generally accurate, the language is clear, and the content is complete. There may be slight imperfections in expression or minor inaccuracies in imaging interpretation, but overall quality is high. - 3: The dialogue is accurate and complete, but there may be slight room for improvement in certain areas. For example, the imaging interpretation could be clearer, or language expression could be slightly more refined. Overall quality is good and suitable for training data. - 4: The dialogue contains some notable medical errors or unclear language that affects accuracy. The imaging interpretation is problematic, or it does not integrate well with other clinical information, leading to incomplete content and a reduction in quality. - 5: The dialogue contains errors or is unclear, potentially misleading medical decisions. There is a lack of effective imaging interpretation, the information is incomplete, and the language is unclear, which compromises the medical diagnosis. The overall quality is poor. Output Format: Please output the results in JSON format as follows: \{"score": "Quality score", "comments": "the comments for the quality criteria",\} \end{tabular}
elements.sent-2411-395: To ensure the quality of the generated image-text pairs, we propose a systematic evaluation process based on a 5-level scoring system.
elements.sent-2411-396: The definition of each scoring level is outlined in the prompt (Table~\ref{tab:quality ).}
elements.sent-2411-397: The primary objective of this process is to assess the quality of image-text pairs from a collection of 219 generated datasets and 42 publicly available open-source datasets.
elements.sent-2411-398: To ensure the representativeness and scientific rigor of the evaluation, we adopt a sampling survey methodology.
elements.sent-2411-399: Specifically, we randomly select a subset of image-text pairs from each dataset for quality assessment, from which we infer the overall quality of the entire dataset.
elements.sent-2411-400: This sampling survey strategy enables us to efficiently and scientifically evaluate the quality of the image-text pairs, maximizing the reliability and accuracy of the results while significantly reducing the manual review workload.
elements.sent-2411-401: The evaluation process is conducted in multiple stages:.
elements.sent-2411-402: Initial Quality Assessment: From each dataset, 30 random image-text pairs are selected for evaluation.
elements.sent-2411-403: The first step involves an initial quality screening using a multimodal large language model (such as GPT-4o) to automatically assess the quality of the pairs.
elements.sent-2411-404: The model utilizes a predefined prompt, as illustrated in Table~\ref{tab:quality , to assign preliminary quality scores based on the defined criteria (accuracy of medical information, clarity and fluency of language, completeness of the dialogue, and relevance to medical imaging).}
elements.sent-2411-405: Expert Review: Following the model's initial assessment, the 30 image-text pairs are then reviewed by five medical imaging experts.
elements.sent-2411-406: These experts validate the preliminary scores and provide their independent judgments on the quality of the pairs, ensuring that the evaluation aligns with current medical standards and practices.
elements.sent-2411-407: Final Scoring: After expert review, the final score for each dataset is determined based on the consensus of the expert evaluations.
elements.sent-2411-408: If the average score of the 30 image-text pairs from a dataset is less than or equal to 3 (on a scale of 1 to 5), the dataset is classified as having satisfactory quality.
elements.sent-2411-409: Conclusion: After rigorous evaluation, 95\% (247/261) of the datasets met the predefined quality standards and were deemed acceptable.
elements.sent-2411-410: Our analysis showed that most datasets generated by GPT using the given labels exhibited high quality, with only a few requiring prompt optimization and additional information to meet the desired standards.
elements.sent-2411-411: For low-quality datasets, we addressed the issues by refining the prompt design, improving label specificity, and incorporating additional contextual information to ensure the generated quality met the requirements.
elements.sent-2411-412: In contrast, 14 open-release multimodal medical datasets, such as, PMC-OA\cite{lin2023pmc , quilt-1m\cite{ikezogwo2023quilt , often extracted from research papers or manually converted, exhibited various issues such as incomplete dialogues, misalignment between images and text, overly brief responses, inclusion of non-medical images, and poor image clarity.}}
elements.sent-2411-413: Due to these limitations, only a portion of the open-source datasets met the required quality standards.
elements.sent-2411-414: This systematic approach ensures that only high-quality image-text pairs are included for further use in medical imaging research, with clear documentation of the evaluation process and criteria.
